info.descr: Synology Drive Client is a desktop utility for Synology Drive Server that lets you sync and share files between a centralized Synology storage system and multiple computers.

all_versions.content:
Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed multiple security vulnerabilities.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed multiple security vulnerabilities.

Fixed Issues

General

  1. Fixed an issue where Synology Drive Client could unexpectedly close during file syncing if it encountered files with abnormal metadata.
  2. Fixed an issue where downloading a remote folder could fail if parent folders in the local target path did not exist. Starting with this update, the system automatically creates the required directory structure.
  3. Fixed an issue where removing folders from a backup task or deleting an entire backup task might fail.

Windows

  1. Fixed an issue where the Synology Drive Client installer couldn't start properly on some computers.
  2. Fixed an issue where changing only the letter case of a filename could incorrectly trigger a conflict file.
  3. Fixed an issue where syncing or backing up an entire disk on Windows could fail with a folder sync error.

macOS

  1. Fixed an issue where syncing a large number of files in macOS On-demand Sync tasks could cause the computer to slow down or temporarily lose its internet connection.
  2. Fixed an issue where files could not be uploaded during on-demand sync tasks on macOS.

Important Note

  1. This update has been recalled on January 14, 2026, due to an issue where syncing or backing up an entire disk on Windows could fail with a folder sync error. The issue is fixed in version 4.0.2-17889.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where Synology Drive Client could not properly sync files on exFAT file systems in Windows.

What's New

  1. Added support for file locking to prevent conflicts from concurrent edits through manual or automatic locking.
  2. Improved the sync mechanism by storing sync signature files in the sync folder, reducing local computer drive space usage.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where some file changes were not detected, affecting sync accuracy.
  2. Fixed an issue where files from folders deselected for sync still appeared in upload/download logs after restarting the Synology Drive Client.
  3. Fixed an issue where Synology Drive Client might fail to resume downloads if the temporary file was missing.
  4. Fixed an issue where users required to enable 2FA could still log in to Synology Drive Client without setting it up.
  5. Fixed an issue where syncing large folders could trigger unnecessary uploads, leading to slower performance or higher resource usage.
  6. Fixed an issue where files were not synced if only the modification time had changed.
  7. Fixed an issue where users could continue using Synology Drive Client after their password expired.
  8. Fixed an issue where generated thumbnails could be larger than the original images, causing unnecessary space usage.

macOS

What's New

  1. Added support for selecting a custom location for the macOS On-demand Sync folder shortcut, providing more flexibility in accessing synced data.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where Synology Drive Client repeatedly requested folder access permissions when launched on macOS 15 or later.
  2. Fixed an issue where the temp folder was not automatically cleared when the desktop utility closed, causing unnecessary space usage.

Fixed Issues

macOS

  1. Fixed an issue where the On-demand Sync task folder in Finder was not automatically removed after uninstalling Synology Drive Client.

What's New

  1. Added RESTful API support, allowing developers to integrate file management and sharing features into their workflows. Utilize private cloud storage to build secure, efficient, and flexible custom solutions. For details, visit the Synology Office Suite API Documentation.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed a security vulnerability. (Synology-SA-24:21)

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed a security vulnerability. (Synology-SA-24:21)

Fixed Issues

General

  1. Fixed an issue where the character ":" in IPv6 connection was incorrectly interpreted as a URL, leading to resolution failures.
  2. Fixed an issue where file download events might continue to display every time the user restarted the client application.
  3. Optimized the automatic reconnection of QuickConnect after a connection failure.

Windows

  1. Fixed an issue where mass deployment installations might fail on Windows 64-bit versions.

macOS

  1. Fixed an issue where an error could occur during the download process when using On-demand Sync with pinned folders.

What's New

Windows

  1. Added native support for 64-bit OS version of Windows, providing more secure and stable file synchronization.
  2. Improved the stability of syncing large files in On-demand Sync tasks.

Fixed Issues

General

  1. Fixed an issue where editing 0 byte files could cause file conflicts.
  2. Fixed an issue where the backup status might not correctly display the transfer speed.
  3. Fixed an issue where cache files were not automatically cleared after uploading thumbnails, leading to increased space usage.

macOS

  1. Fixed an issue where subsequent operations might not be executed properly following a sync task failure.
  2. Fixed an issue where long file names in the bundle file might cause upload failures.

Windows

  1. Fixed an issue where an unexpected refresh of files occurred in File Explorer.
  2. Fixed an issue where multiple file versions could prevent downloading historical versions or result in inaccurate content in those downloads.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where thumbnails might not be properly displayed when syncing pictures on Windows OS.

What's New

General

  1. Supports displaying video thumbnails for videos that were uploaded to the server from the client.
  2. Supports wiping synced files from the clients when users unlink these clients in Synology Drive Admin Console.
  3. Reduced the length of time to initialize and close the client when there was a large number of tasks.
  4. Refined the setup page of the installation wizard for a more simplified and user-friendly onboarding experience.
  5. Supports excluding unsynced logs for reasons such as sync limitations or custom rules when filtering logs.
  6. Supports automatically searching for nearby NAS devices in the same area when establishing a connection.

macOS

  1. Optimized the power consumption of Synology Drive Client on macOS devices.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where syncing might fail due to long file names, which were the result of automatic system rename during file conflict resolution.
  2. Minor bug fixes.

Compatibility and Installation

  1. Starting from the next version, software installation or update will no longer be supported for Windows 7 and Ubuntu 18.04.

What's New

General

  1. Improved the execution efficiency of backup tasks.
  2. Improved sync efficiency and reduced network usage for sync tasks with a lot of small files.

macOS

  1. The updated tray icon automatically changes between black and white depending on the shade of the device's desktop wallpaper.

Fixed issues

  1. Fixed an issue where On-demand Sync tasks might fail to sync properly on macOS.
  2. Minor bug fixes.

Fixed issues

  1. Fixed an issue where the auto-update mechanism for the Synology Drive Client for Windows version could not function properly.

What's New

macOS

  1. Supports the "Free up space" option on On-demand Sync on macOS, which allows for releasing local space occupied by downloaded files in the folder with one-click.

Fixed issues

  1. Fixed an issue where the search icon used for locating servers in Backup Task > Settings > Connection is unresponsive.
  2. Fixed an issue where macOS users restarting Synology Drive Client/rebooting computer might cause the local sync folder of the On-demand Sync task to disappear.
  3. Fixed an issue where the icon overlays of sync tasks could not be displayed properly on Ubuntu 22.10.
  4. Minor bug fixes.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where updating Synology Drive Client to 3.2 version from 3.1 would fail if users did not have any tasks set up.
  2. Fixed an issue where, after updating to macOS 13, Synology Drive Client would fail to initialize if there are tasks using external drives as destination folders. To ensure that your sync or backup tasks function properly, we recommend not using external devices as destination folders.
  3. Fixed an issue where users could not remove tags from local files for On-demand Sync tasks on macOS.
  4. Fixed an issue where the "Done" button was not functioning properly when users created On-demand Sync tasks on macOS.
  5. Fixed an issue where Synology Drive Client on Linux computers did not trust valid certificates.

Compatibility and Installation

  1. Starting from this version, software installation or upgrade will no longer be supported for macOS 10.13 and Ubuntu 32-bit.

What's New

General

  1. Added support to filter logs of files that were unsynced.

Windows

  1. Supports mass deployment of Synology Drive Client through Active Directory Server.

macOS

  1. Added support for the On-demand Sync function on sync tasks to conserve local storage space. This function is only supported on macOS 12.3 and above.
  2. Added native support for Mac models with Apple Silicon without the need to install Rosetta 2.
  3. Integrated with Mac notifications to provide native user experience.

Fixed issues

  1. Fixed an issue on Mac and Linux systems where Synology Drive Client would crash when user clicked the Backup Rule button in Backup Task.

Important Note

  1. This update was recalled on October 26, 2022 to fix an issue where Synology Drive Client on Mac and Linux systems would crash when user clicked the Backup Rule button in Backup Task. The issue is fixed in version 3.2.0-13238.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where connecting to the server via QuickConnect might consume too much CPU resources and slow down upload and download speeds.
  2. Fixed an issue where the backup task might unexpectedly create an extra "/Drive'' layer under home/Drive.
  3. Fixed an issue where connecting to a server that uses any SSL certificates other than the default one might fail.

Compatibility & Installation

  1. This is the last version to support macOS 10.13 and Ubuntu 32-bit.

What's New

  1. Supports syncing files with the "home" folder. This requires connecting to Synology Drive Server 3.1 Beta and above that has expanded the indexing scope to the entire "/home".

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where the application could not run on macOS 10.13.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where changing the SSL certificate when connecting via QuickConnect might cause syncing to fail.
  2. Fixed an issue where sync tasks might become stuck when Microsoft Defender was enabled.
  3. Fixed an issue where backup tasks could not be completed successfully.
  4. Fixed an issue where sync tasks that include sub-folders might not be completed successfully.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where syncing a large number of files with thumbnails via On-demand Sync might cause the application to shut down unexpectedly.
  2. Fixed multiple issues that might have caused file syncing to fail.
  3. Fixed an issue where antivirus software triggered by deleting or renaming files might cause sync tasks to fail.
  4. Fixed an issue where insufficient disk space on the computer might cause the application to shut down unexpectedly.
  5. Fixed minor bugs.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed an issue where files modified and then renamed on the server while Synology Drive Client is disconnected will not be synced to the local computer once the application reconnects.
  2. Fixed an issue where sync tasks with On-demand Sync mode might cause the application to shut down unexpectedly.
  3. Fixed an issue where files renamed and then modified on the server while Synology Drive Client is disconnected cannot be opened properly if the files have enabled On-demand Sync.

Bug Fixed

  1. Fixed an issue where pausing and then resuming file uploads from Synology Drive Client creates temp files that could not be removed.
  2. Fixed an issue where syncing with the server might fail when downloading folders that contain files.
  3. Fixed an issue where syncing might fail when syncing folders that once had been filtered out by sync rules.

Important Note

  1. This update fixed an issue in Synology Drive 3.0 where files might be accidentally deleted if On-demand Sync mode is turned on while updating from Drive 2.0 to Drive 3.0.

Compatibility and Installation

  1. Updated to be compatible with DSM 7.0.
  2. This version no longer supports connection to Cloud Station Server. The connected Cloud Station Server must be updated to the latest version of Synology Drive Sever for compatibility.

What's New

  1. Supports displaying personal quota usage in shared folders.
  2. Improved checking the path of Volume Shadow Copy service when making backup copies on Windows to meet higher security levels.
  3. Improved sync performance on Synology Drive Client when renaming files or folders on Synology NAS.
  4. Improved the welcome tutorial to provide better usage guidance.
  5. Simplified the setup flow for enhanced user experience, including moving the setup for "Shared with me" to the "Edit Connection" button on the Task Menu.

Important Note

This version has been replaced by Synology Drive Client 3.0.1 to fix an issue where files might be accidentally deleted if On-demand Sync mode is turned on while updating from Drive 2.0 to Drive 3.0.

Bug fixed

  1. Fixed an issue where files under On-demand Sync might not be successfully deleted from the server if the modified time had been updated.
  2. Fixed an issue where the SSL certificate might become untrusted if users change the certificate and connect again via QuickConnect.
  3. Fixed an issue where deleting sync tasks with On-demand Sync enabled might fail if the user quits the app during the deletion process.
  4. Fixed an issue where the Menu Bar Icons might not display correctly on macOS 10.15 and above versions.
  5. Fixed an issue where the incorrect server address might be inserted when using the "Search" icon in the "Edit Connection" window.
  6. Fixed the calendar display issue in the "Public Link" window.

Bug Fixed

  1. Fixed the issue where Synology Drive Client might fail to launch on macOS 11.
  2. Fixed the issue where Synology Drive Client might quit unexpectedly on macOS when the computer wakes up from sleep mode.
  3. Fixed the issue where synchronization might fail when the number of files to synchronize exceeds 100,000.
  4. Fixed the issue where Synology Drive Client must be relaunched to apply changes made to the backup folder's settings.

Bug fixed

  1. Fixed the issue where files might be rescanned every time Synology Drive Client is launched.
  2. Fixed the issue where backup tasks might be erased when upgrading from Cloud Station Drive and Cloud Station Backup to Synology Drive Client simultaneously.
  3. Minor bug fixes.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed the issue where connection to Synology Drive Server might fail if the server does not correctly recognize the IP address.
  2. Fixed the issue where Synology Drive Client might not function properly if the user's home directory file path is renamed on the local computer.
  3. Fixed the issue where Synology Drive Client might not function properly if the user's home directory file path is renamed on the local computer.
  4. Fixed the issue where continuously modifying files within a short period might cause the package to quit unexpectedly.
  5. Fixed the issue where syncing tasks might freeze when syncing files with preview-only permission.
  6. Fixed the issue where file tags on macOS might not be synced when version control for these files is not enabled.
  7. Fixed the issue where renaming files might fail if there are file conflicts on the Windows system.
  8. Fixed the issue where establishing backup tasks under unstable connection might result in a Synology Drive Server version outdated error message.
  9. Fixed the issue where installing incompatible versions of OpenSSL from other applications on macOS might cause the package to quit unexpectedly.
  10. Fixed the issue where Synology Office files might not be synced to the latest version under On-demand Sync mode if they are moved during syncing.
  11. Fixed the issue where copying and pasting Synology Office files under On-demand Sync mode might cause sync tasks to freeze.
  12. Fixed a security vulnerability.
  13. Fixed the issue where deleting files right after creating them might fail in macOS.
  14. Fixed the issue where Microsoft Excel temp files might be synced to the server.

Fixed Issues

  1. Fixed the issue where Synology Drive folder shortcuts on the left panel in File Explorer might be duplicated after upgrading to 2.0 version and enabling On-demand Sync.
  2. Fixed the issue where continuous file modifications within a short period of time might cause synchronization failure.
  3. Fixed the issue where upgrading from Cloud Station might fail if the application was improperly shut down on Linux.
  4. Fixed the issue where shared folder without versioning might cause synchronization failure.
  5. Fixed the issue where scheduled backup tasks continue to run after designated end time.
  6. Fixed the issue where backing up large files might cause incomplete file backup.
  7. Fixed the issue where saving Microsoft Excel files might create temp files when On-demand Sync is enabled.

Compatibility & Installation

  1. Synology Drive Client 2.0 will be gradually rolling out to global users.
  2. After installing Synology Drive Client 2.0 , Cloud Station Drive and/or Cloud Station Backup will be replaced and safely migrated to Synology Drive Client. (Learn more)

What’s New

  1. Aligned display date and time formats with device system setting.
  2. Added support for Thai interface.

Below new features requires Synology Drive Server 2.0 or above:

  1. Synology Drive Client now comes with brand new computer backup function and supports continuous, manual, and scheduled modes. Built-in restore window allows for granular file recovery to version at certain point of time.
  2. Added On-demand Sync on Windows 10 platform to effortlessly browse files and download files only when needed. (Learn more)
  3. Supports disallowing the downloading and copying of files and their contents when users share files.
  4. Supports granular permission roles for file collaboration.
  5. Supports “Get link” option for synced files.

1. Optimized compatibility with macOS 64-bit architecture.
  2. Added support to automatically adjust date and time format according to the device’s operating system settings.
  3. Fixed an issue where the file path of the synced file might be incorrect on Windows platforms.
  4. Fixed an issue where using category filter in Logs might cause inaccurate search results.
  5. Fixed an issue where the application might crash when syncing icns files on macOS platforms.
  6. Minor bug fixes.
Minor bug fixes.

1. Supports 64-bit macOS client requiring macOS 10.11 and onward.

  2. Fixed an issue where syncing a large number of files might cause a client to crash.

  3. Fixed an issue where synchronization tasks might loop after renaming a large number of files when shutting down the application.

  4. Fixed an issue where synchronization tasks might loop when synced folders contain an empty .lnk file or a .lnk file linking to a large number of files.

  5. Fixed an issue where synchronization tasks might fail to run after adjusting/enabling/disabling the file filter.

  6. Minor bug fixes.

1. Fixed an issue where directories might not be able to be deleted after setting selective synchronization rules.

  2. Fixed an issue where restarting an application might take too much time after filtering a large number of files in a selective synchronization task.

  3. Fixed an issue where synchronization might loop after shutting down service and renaming or moving folders with a large number of files at the same time.

  4. Fixed an issue where an application might crash after moving files to the folder set for sub-folder synchronization task.

What’s new

  1. Enhanced the performance of transferring modified data. 

The following feature requires Drive 1.1.0 and above versions:

  1. Supports advanced protection links to add password and validity period for public file sharing.

Fixed issues

  1. Fixed an issue where user might need to manually reconnect the application to the server when its SSL certificate is renewed.

  2. Fixed an issue where application may crash when the signature file is larger than memory size.

  3. Fixed an issue where synchronization task might be looping.

  4. Fixed an issue where continuous file modifications on Mac device might cause synchronization failure and client crash.

  5. Minor bug fixes.

1. Fixed an issue where continuous file modifications on Mac device might cause synchronization failure and client crash.
  2. Fixed an issue where synchronization task might be looping.
  3. Fixed an issue where filtering large number of files might cause synchronization tasks to fail.
  4. Fixed an issue where synchronization tasks set in shared folders with ACL read-only permission might fail to upload files to the server.
  5. Minor bug fixes.

1. Fixed an issue where files might fail to be deleted on the client side.
  2. Minor bug fixes.

• Fixed an issue where syncing large numbers of files from the Drive server might cause high CPU usage.
  • Fixed an issue where file sharing links might fail to be created on macOS devices when the system language is Czech.
  • Minor bug fixes.

• Supports syncing files and folders between PCs and Synology Drive.
  • Supports selectively syncing files and folders in My Drive, Team Folders and Shared with me.
  • Supports desktop notifications and in-app browsing of file sharing and comments insertion history.
  • Supports creating sharing links with configurable permission settings.
  • Supports multiple sync modes: one-way upload, one-way download and bi-directional sync.


Note

When Cloud Station Drive is upgraded to Drive, existing files and folders will not be synced again. However, the system might need some time to reindex the database.
